mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing required returns and lifting S&P 500 valuations. A 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals monetary accommodation that produces positive forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.38% as of 2016-02-15 reflects post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives retail sales and corporate earnings expectations. A 3-month acceleration in PAYEMS growth produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,560k as of 2016-02-15 reflects post-recession labor recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with sustained job growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following PAYEMS acceleration periods do not exceed baseline by at least 2% on average.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both series rise together, demand-side momentum accelerates, supporting corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,099k and RSAFS at 447,308M as of 2016-01-15 show demand resilience
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns when both HOUST and RSAFS rise are not significantly higher than when only one rises (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression. This triggers negative equity returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward in response to deteriorating manufacturing activity.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.85 as of 2016-01-15 reflects post-crisis industrial recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines below 3-month MA are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE crosses above this threshold, equity investors reduce growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-02-15 is near the 5.0% threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods when UNRATE > 5.0% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% combined with FEDFUNDS below 1.0% signals real monetary accommodation that inflates asset prices. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.1 and FEDFUNDS at 0.38% as of 2016-01-15 reflect low real rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when both conditions hold are not significantly higher than when only one holds (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured as quarter-over-quarter acceleration signals strengthening economic momentum. When GDPC1 growth accelerates above 2.0% annualized, equity investors increase earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth accelerates above 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,455.1B as of 2016-10-15 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining simultaneously signal broad-based economic contraction affecting both supply capacity and labor demand. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors recognize recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show post-crisis recovery as of 2016-02-15
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction or recession onset
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns when both conditions hold are not significantly negative compared to when only one holds (t-test p > 0.15).
